content.full_text:
September 10, 1982
Cromberg, California

F P P C

Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 "K" Street
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Sirs;

Enclosed please find a copy of "Conflict of Interest Code" drafted by
the Plumas County Arts Commission. In July, 1982, I spoke with a man
in your office's legal division about the practice of the Plumas County
Arts Commission's hiring a member of the commission, Janet Gogue. I
also was advised by your office to contact Jeff Fuller in the offices
of the Attorney General. I did so.

Based on what I understand from him, I requested the Commission not to
hire members any longer. Ms. Gogue resigned from the commission that
same afternoon and remained an employee under contract to the commission.
She and apparently the other members of the commission felt this corrected
the conflict. As a result, I also resigned from the commission because
they had failed to withold IRS taxes and because I still felt that there
was a conflict, as the commissioners and Gogue sat down together and
re-drafted her proposal after she had been hired.

Apparently, either I misunderstood the conflict of interest code or the
FPAC does. I would greatly appreciate enlightenment for both of us.

Please mail copies of the laws to  Plumas County Arts Commission
P.O. 613
Quincy, California  95971
Att: Betsy Schramel, Chm.

I would also like to know if the resignation of Gogue corrected the conflict.
On a phone call on 9/4/82, I explained the
provisions of the PRA.

[signature]

Your assistance in this matter is greatly appreciated.

Thank you,


Jean E. Walters

Box 26

Blairsden, California  96103
9/6-836-2577

JEM/km

cc:Jeff Fuller

o/o Attorney General

Sacramento, California

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662           322-5660             322-5901         322-6441           322-6444

September 23, 1982


Jean E. Walters
Box 26
Blairden, CA  96103

Re: A-82-156

Dear Ms. Walters

Your letter requesting advice under the Political
Reform Act has been referred to Janis Shank McLean, an
attorney in the Legal Division of the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact this attorney directly at
(916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly.
Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex
legal questions, or unless more information is needed to
answer your request, you should expect a response within 21
working days.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
Barbara A. Milman
Chief
Legal Division

BAM:plh
Enclosure
